name: crra_tstat_near_floor_wealth
purpose: Pins the W-H4 contract: floor applied to W (input), NOT to U (output). A floor-hitting day (r_policy_pct = -99.99%) produces W_raw = 0.0001 < WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR = 0.001. The W-side floor clamps W to 0.001 before CRRA, yielding U_floor_day = crra(0.001, gamma). Scenarios at gamma=2.0 and gamma=5.0 pin the W-H4 contract across the prudential gamma ceiling (plan §NN1: recommended gamma <= 5.0). The t-stat computed on the W-side-floored series is finite for both gammas.
provenance: Non-circular: W_raw derived from r_policy_pct via W = 1 + r/100. W_floored = max(WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR, W_raw). U = crra(W_floored, gamma) = (W^(1-gamma) - 1)/(1-gamma). All arithmetic computed analytically from the formula; no SUT output used as ground truth.

expected.discriminating_note: W-side floor: U[3] = crra(0.001, gamma=2.0) = -999.0, sd(U) = 377.6. A U-side floor at -10 clips U[3] to -10 and shrinks sd to 3.78 -- a ~100x difference. The test asserts U[3] == crra(WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR, gamma) exactly, proving the floor was on W.

tolerance_notes.U_floor_day: exact equality (-999.0 is an exact IEEE-754 double for 1 - 1/0.001)

expected.U_floor_day_approx_note: spec-m1 Finding 10 quoted u(W=0.5, gamma=5.0) = -4.0 as the illustration; the fixture uses W=WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR=0.001 (the actual floor). At WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR the value is ~-2.5e11, far larger in magnitude than the gamma=2.0 case (-999.0). Both are finite. Note: spec-m1 also omitted the '-1' term giving -4.0; correct value is (16-1)/(-4) = -3.75.
expected.discriminating_note: At gamma=5.0 the floor-hitting day dominates even more than gamma=2.0: U[3] ~ -2.5e11 vs -999.0 at gamma=2.0. The t-stat remains finite (~-1.0) because sd(U) scales proportionally with U[3]. This pins IEEE-754 stability at the prudential gamma ceiling (plan §NN1 recommends gamma <= 5.0).

tolerance_notes.U_floor_day: abs=1e-3 (the value is ~-2.5e11; last-bit floating point at that magnitude is ~1e-4)
